data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Haranga Resources Limited ( (AU:HAR) ).

Haranga Resources Limited has issued 500,000 fully paid ordinary shares following the exercise of an equivalent number of unlisted options at $0.08 each, with the new securities placed without a disclosure document under the relevant Corporations Act provisions. The company confirmed its ongoing compliance with financial reporting and continuous disclosure obligations, and noted that drilling is underway at its Lincoln Gold Project, with results to be analysed and released in line with its market reporting duties.

The share issue marginally increases Haranga’s capital base and reflects the conversion of options into equity, which may modestly strengthen its balance sheet and align option holders more closely with shareholders. Operationally, the reference to the active drilling program at Lincoln underscores continued investment in exploration, with upcoming assay results likely to influence market perceptions of the project’s potential and the company’s growth trajectory.

More about Haranga Resources Limited

Haranga Resources Limited is an ASX-listed resources company focused on mineral exploration and project development, including the Lincoln Gold Project in Australia. The company advances its assets through drilling programs and related exploration activities aimed at defining and growing its resource base in the gold sector.

Average Trading Volume: 1,206,160

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$60.02M

data.title: ImExHS Sets Hybrid AGM for May as It Formalises Digital Governance Approach
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from ImExHS Limited ( (AU:IME) ) is now available.

ImExHS Limited has called its Annual General Meeting for 11:00am AEST on 20 May 2026, to be held as a hybrid event at its Sydney CBD venue and via an online platform operated by Automic, allowing shareholders to attend, vote, and ask questions either in person or virtually. The company is moving away from routine hard-copy distribution of meeting documents in line with Corporations Act amendments, directing investors to access the Notice of Meeting and proxy materials online, while the board unanimously recommends voting in favour of all resolutions and stresses the importance of shareholder participation through poll-based voting.

Shareholders who wish to receive physical documentation can still request hard copies, but otherwise are expected to download the Notice of Meeting and instructions for electronic voting and question submission from the company’s website or ASX platform. By formalising digital access and an online participation structure, ImExHS is aligning its governance practices with updated Australian regulatory settings, potentially improving engagement and efficiency while reinforcing board support for the resolutions to be considered at the AGM.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:IME) stock is a Buy with a A$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ImExHS Limited stock, see the AU:IME Stock Forecast page.

More about ImExHS Limited

ImExHS Limited is an Australia-listed company that provides technology and services to healthcare providers, with a focus on medical imaging and related clinical workflows. Its shareholder base is primarily engaged through ASX disclosures and formal governance processes, including hybrid in-person and virtual meetings to facilitate participation across its investor community.

Average Trading Volume: 28,400

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Electro Optic Systems Holdings Limited ( (AU:EOS) ) has issued an announcement.

Electro Optic Systems Holdings Limited has released its notice of meeting and voting form for the 2026 Annual General Meeting, confirming that the AGM will be held on 19 May 2026 at the offices of law firm MinterEllison in central Sydney. The company’s disclosure formalises logistical details for shareholder participation in governance matters, reinforcing standard corporate oversight and offering investors an opportunity to engage directly with management on strategic and operational issues.

The announcement, authorised by the chief executive officer, underscores EOS’s focus on transparent communication with the market and compliance with Australian listing requirements. For shareholders, the scheduled AGM serves as a key forum to review the company’s performance in its defence and space systems businesses, vote on resolutions, and gauge management’s outlook in sectors driven by defence spending and growing demand for space situational awareness.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:EOS) stock is a Buy with a A$12.95 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Electro Optic Systems Holdings Limited stock, see the AU:EOS Stock Forecast page.

More about Electro Optic Systems Holdings Limited

Electro Optic Systems Holdings Limited operates across Defence Systems and Space Systems, focusing on advanced military and space technologies. Its defence division provides weapon systems optimisation, remote weapon stations, vehicle turrets, high‑energy laser weapons, counter‑UAS solutions and integrated ISR and C4 systems for land warfare. The space division uses proprietary optical sensors and effectors to detect, track and characterise objects in space, supporting broader space control capabilities.

data.title: KMD Brands directors boost stakes amid entitlement offer
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

KMD Brands Limited ( (AU:KMD) ) has shared an announcement.

KMD Brands has disclosed that non-executive director Zion Armstrong significantly increased his beneficial holding in the company through participation in a recent placement, lifting his stake from about 119,157 shares to more than 618,324 shares. The shares were acquired for roughly NZ$29,950 at NZ$0.06 per share as part of KMD’s pro rata accelerated entitlement offer launched on 31 March 2026.

The company confirmed that all KMD directors who are existing shareholders intend to take up their entitlements in the offer on a pro rata basis, with chair Philip Bowman and director David Kirk committing to apply for at least double their pro rata allocations in the retail AREO. Further disclosure notices for other directors are expected after the retail entitlement allotment on 28 April 2026, underscoring strong board support for the capital-raising initiative and its strategic objectives.

More about KMD Brands Limited

KMD Brands Limited is a listed outdoor and lifestyle apparel company, with its ordinary shares traded on both the NZX and ASX under the ticker KMD. The group focuses on branded clothing and equipment for outdoor, adventure, and active consumers across Australasian and international markets, operating through well-known retail and wholesale channels.

Duxton Water Ltd. ( (AU:RIV) ) just unveiled an update.

Rivco Australia Limited has released documentation for its FY2025 Annual General Meeting, outlining that the material is intended as general information rather than a comprehensive disclosure of the company’s financial position or prospects. The company stresses that the document is not a prospectus or product disclosure statement and that investors should rely on Rivco’s broader ASX filings and seek independent advice before making investment decisions.

Heavy Minerals Ltd ( (AU:HVY) ) has provided an update.

Heavy Minerals Limited has lodged a notice of a proposed issue of unquoted options with the ASX, outlining a placement or similar type of securities issue scheduled for 17 April 2026. The company plans to issue up to 325,000 unquoted options across several new classes, each with different exercise prices and expiry dates in early 2028, signalling an incentive or capital management initiative that could influence future capital structure and potential dilution for existing shareholders.

The proposed options comprise 25,000 options exercisable at $0.8786 expiring on 31 January 2028, and three tranches of 100,000 options each, exercisable at $0.8585, $0.8547 and $0.8488 with February 2028 expiries. This structured grant of unquoted options may be aimed at aligning interests of key stakeholders with long-term company performance and provides additional leverage to future share price appreciation without immediate cash inflow, while expanding the pool of potential equity on exercise.
